reasoning: Lilly King has held the 100m breaststroke (long course) world record (1:04.13) since July 2017. As of the knowledge cutoff (October 26, 2025), this record remains standing. Since long course world records are extremely rare outside of major summer international championships or primary national trials (usually held in the spring), the probability of the record being broken in this winter window is very low. The gradual decrease in probability across the three dates reflects the increasing (though still low) cumulative window for an unexpected record-breaking performance at a smaller or domestic long course meet.
